content.claim: Mainstream school leaders informally self-limit SNI intake based on perceived institutional capacity, a safeguard-like behavior that exists at the school level but is not centrally monitored, audited uniformly, or documented in policy.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: This finding is based on a single dissertation recommendation rather than a systematic audit of school-level rationing practices
The prevalence of informal SNI-intake limitation across Hungarian schools is not quantified
The equity implications (e.g., which schools absorb more SNI pupils) and any central oversight of this informal rationing are not established
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
